title: dryopteris intermedia subsp. azorica
intro: Dryopteris intermedia subsp. azorica, also known as Azores golden-scaled male fern, is a subspecies of fern native to the Azores Islands in Portugal. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Dryopteris intermedia subsp. azorica can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
